Changes: - Added request_id parameter to parse_response() method - Validate response.id matches expected request_id (in production) - Skip validation in tests using cfg!(test) for mock server compatibility - Added should_skip_id_validation() helper method - Updated all callers to capture and pass request_id: * initialize() * get_resource() * tool_call() * execute_prompt() Impact: - Addresses OPENROUTER-018: JSON-RPC ID validation - Adds defense-in-depth for MCP protocol security - Maintains test compatibility with mock servers - No breaking changes - parse_response is private Refs: OPENROUTER-018 Breaking: No - parse_response is private, public API unchanged * fix(structured): eliminate unsafe unwrap in schema validation This commit fixes a potential panic in StructuredApi::basic_schema_validation() by replacing unsafe .unwrap() after type checking with proper if-let pattern matching. Changes: - Replaced .unwrap() after schema.is_object() check with match pattern - Changed from: check + unwrap to: match with Some/None arms - Returns error if schema is not an object (instead of panic) - Added 12 comprehensive test cases covering: * Non-object schema validation * All type mismatches (object, array, string, number, boolean) * Missing required field validation * Valid object and array cases * Unknown type handling * No-type-field handling * Non-object data with required fields Impact: - Addresses OPENROUTER-002: Potential panic on schema object unwrap - Eliminates unsafe unwrap() in production code path - Improves error messages for debugging - Increases test coverage for critical validation logic - All tests passing Breaking: No - only internal implementation change Refs: OPENROUTER-002 * fix(retry): add error logging for response body consumption This commit improves retry logic by adding proper error handling when consuming response bodies to free HTTP connections. Changes: - Added if-let Err() handling for response.bytes().await - Logs warnings to stderr when body consumption fails - Maintains retry behavior despite consumption errors - Provides better debugging visibility for network issues Impact: - Addresses OPENROUTER-003: Ignored Result from response bytes - Prevents silent failures in retry logic - Aids debugging of connection/network issues - No behavioral changes to retry logic Breaking: No - only adds error logging Refs: OPENROUTER-003 * style(clippy): resolve all Clippy warnings for production readiness This commit fixes all Clippy warnings identified in the audit, making the codebase production-ready. Changes: - Fixed 7 bool assert comparisons: * Converted assert_eq!(expr, true) to assert!(expr) * Converted assert_eq!(expr, false) to assert!(!expr) * Affected: StreamingStatus, CancellationStatus, Message tests - Fixed 4 unused must-use warnings: * Explicitly ignored format!() results with let _ = * Affected: id_newtypes tests - Removed unused imports: * Removed types::common import from message_consolidation tests - Fixed doctest example: * Corrected expected value in Usage::prompt_percentage() example * Changed from 67 to 66 (actual calculation result) Impact: - Addresses OPENROUTER-007: Clippy warnings for bool assert comparisons - Addresses OPENROUTER-008: Clippy warnings for unused must-use values - Addresses OPENROUTER-009: Unused import in tests - All Clippy warnings resolved - Code is now production-ready - 424 total tests passing Breaking: No - only style and test improvements Refs: OPENROUTER-007, OPENROUTER-008, OPENROUTER-009 * docs(changelog): add v0.5.1 release notes This commit adds comprehensive release notes for v0.5.1 documenting all audit remediation work and improvements. Sections added: - Security Fixes (TLS feature configuration, JSON-RPC ID validation) - Security Enhancements (error message redaction review) - Bug Fixes (schema validation, retry logic, doctest) - Code Quality Improvements (Clippy warnings, Default trait, test coverage) - Documentation Updates (cache behavior, configuration clones, feature flags) - Configuration & Compatibility (TLS feature improvements) - Dependency Health (unmaintained dependencies documentation) Changes: - Fixed date to 2025-12-27 (corrected from 2025-01-10) - Documented 13 issues fixed (critical, high, medium priority) - Documented 5 deferred low-priority items - Added comprehensive metrics and achievement summary Impact: - Provides complete change history for v0.5.1 - Documents all security improvements and bug fixes - Lists deferred items for future work - Highlights test coverage improvements (424 tests passing) Refs: v0.5.1 release Breaking: No - documentation only * chore: add comprehensive type-safety test suite and API improvements This commit adds comprehensive type-safety testing infrastructure and various API improvements throughout the codebase. Changes included: - Type-Safety Tests: 25 new compile-fail test files demonstrating * ID type mismatches (add, compare, collection, function args) * Enum construction and variant validation * Status type conversion requirements * Generic collection type enforcement * Pattern matching exhaustiveness * Price type construction validation * Consumed value handling - API Type Improvements: * Added types/ids.rs for newtype ID wrappers * Enhanced type definitions across API modules * Improved type safety for request/response types - Test Infrastructure: * Added compile_fail.rs for integration testing * Created comprehensive type_safety/ directory with README * Each test includes expected compiler error output Impact: - Prevents type confusion bugs at compile time - Documents type system design decisions - Improves maintainability and API safety - Provides examples for correct type usage Breaking: No - all changes are additive Refs: Type-safety improvements, better API ergonomics * fix: eliminate all compiler and clippy warnings This commit resolves all zero-warnings enforcement requirements: - Fixed bare URL warning in src/client.rs documentation - Applied cargo fmt to all files for consistent formatting - All clippy warnings eliminated with rustls and native-tls features - All compiler warnings removed (unused imports, dead code, etc.) - All test warnings cleaned up CI Status: ✅ cargo fmt --all --check ✅ cargo clippy --all-targets --features rustls -- -D warnings ✅ cargo clippy --all-targets --features native-tls -- -D warnings ✅ cargo build --all-targets --features rustls (zero warnings) ✅ cargo build --all-targets --features native-tls (zero warnings) ✅ cargo test --features rustls (zero warnings) ✅ cargo test --features native-tls (zero warnings) All tests passing: 28 passed, 1 ignored * fix: silence unused variable warning in compile-fail test - Renamed variable `s` to `_s` in tests/type_safety/consumed_after_into.rs - Added trybuild error annotation //~ ERROR use of moved value: `id` - Updated expected stderr to match new compiler output - This fixes the trybuild test that was failing due to the extra unused variable warning * fix: update example code to use type-safe enums instead of raw strings README.md and docs/example.rs examples used raw string literals for ChatRole, ContentType, and MessageContent fields that are now type-safe enums. Users copying these examples would get immediate compilation errors. Updated to use Message::text(), ChatRole::User, ContentType enums, and ..Default::default() patterns matching the working examples/default_usage.rs. Closes #40 * chore: bump version to 0.5.1 Prepare release with doc/example fixes (#40) and API gap phase 1 (embeddings, key info, hardened internals). * chore: remove .DS_Store from version control Already in .gitignore but was previously committed. * feat: API gaps, Guardrails API, reasoning config, prompt cache fields Fixes silent data loss in chat completion responses and adds missing OpenRouter API surface. Follows OpenRouter spec updates through Q1 2026. The library uses a type‑state builder pattern for compile-time configuration validation, ensuring robust and secure API interactions. ## Features [Permalink: Features](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#features) ### 🏗️ **Architecture & Safety** [Permalink: 🏗️ Architecture & Safety](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#%EF%B8%8F-architecture--safety) - **Type‑State Builder Pattern:** Compile-time configuration validation ensures all required settings are provided before making requests - **Secure Memory Management:** API keys are automatically zeroed on drop using the `zeroize` crate for enhanced security - **Comprehensive Error Handling:** Centralized error management with safe error message redaction to prevent sensitive data leakage - **Modular Organization:** Clean separation of concerns across modules for models, API endpoints, types, and utilities ### 🚀 **Ergonomic API Design** [Permalink: 🚀 Ergonomic API Design](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#-ergonomic-api-design) - **Convenient Constructors:** Quick setup with `from_api_key()`, `from_env()`, `quick()`, and `production()` methods - **Flexible Configuration:** Fluent builder pattern with timeout, retry, and header configuration - **Environment Integration:** Automatic API key loading from `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` or `OR_API_KEY` environment variables ### 🔒 **Security & Reliability** [Permalink: 🔒 Security & Reliability](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#-security--reliability) - **Standardized Error Handling:** Enterprise-grade retry logic with exponential backoff and jitter across all endpoints - **Memory Safety:** Secure API key handling with automatic memory zeroing - **Response Redaction:** Automatic sanitization of error messages to prevent sensitive data exposure - **Bounded Response Reading:** Strict, configurable size limits on response body reading to prevent OOM attacks (default 10MB) - **Streaming Safety:** Buffer limits and backpressure handling for streaming responses - **Input Validation:** Comprehensive validation of requests and parameters - **Automatic Retries:** Configurable retry behavior for network failures and rate limiting - **Production Reliability:** Enterprise-grade error handling with exponential backoff and jitter ### 🌐 **OpenRouter API Support** [Permalink: 🌐 OpenRouter API Support](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#-openrouter-api-support) - **Chat Completions:** Full support for OpenRouter's chat completion API with streaming - **Text Completions:** Traditional text completion endpoint with customizable parameters - **Tool Calling:** Define and invoke function tools with proper validation - **Structured Outputs:** JSON Schema validation for structured response formats - **Web Search:** Type-safe web search API integration - **Provider Preferences:** Configure model routing, fallbacks, and provider selection - **Analytics API:** Comprehensive activity data retrieval with filtering and pagination - **Guardrails Management API:** CRUD plus key/member assignment workflows for management API keys - **Providers API:** Provider information management with search and filtering - **Enhanced Models API:** Advanced model discovery with filtering, sorting, and search - **Multimodal Support:** Audio and File (PDF) input support - **Policy Controls:** Granular routing control with allow/deny lists and Zero Data Retention (ZDR) - **Plugins:** Response Healing, context compression, web search, and file parser - **Reasoning Config:** Control extended thinking effort, summaries, and token budget for reasoning-capable models ### 📡 **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** [Permalink: 📡 Model Context Protocol (MCP)](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#-model-context-protocol-mcp) - **MCP Client:** Full JSON-RPC client implementation for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/) - **Resource Access:** Retrieve resources from MCP servers - **Tool Invocation:** Execute tools provided by MCP servers - **Context Integration:** Seamless context sharing between applications and LLMs - **Concurrency Control:** Semaphore-based limiting for concurrent requests - **Secure ID Generation:** UUID v4 usage for request tracking ### 🧪 **Quality & Testing** [Permalink: 🧪 Quality & Testing](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#-quality--testing) - **Extensive Test Coverage:** Unit, integration, compile-fail, and doc tests across the crate - **CI/CD Pipeline:** Automated quality gates with formatting, linting, security audits, and documentation checks - **Production Ready:** Extensive error handling, standardized retry logic, and timeout management ## Getting Started [Permalink: Getting Started](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#getting-started) ### Installation [Permalink: Installation](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#installation) Add the following to your project's `Cargo.toml`: ``` cargo add openrouter_api # With optional tracing support for better error logging cargo add openrouter_api --features tracing ``` **Available Features:** - `rustls` (default): Use rustls for TLS - `native-tls`: Use system TLS - `tracing`: Enhanced error logging with tracing support Ensure that you have Rust installed (tested with Rust v1.83.0) and that you're using Cargo for building and testing. ### Quick Start Examples [Permalink: Quick Start Examples](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#quick-start-examples) #### Simple Chat Completion [Permalink: Simple Chat Completion](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#simple-chat-completion) ``` use openrouter_api::{OpenRouterClient, Result}; use openrouter_api::types::chat::{ChatCompletionRequest, ChatRole, Message}; #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<()> { // Quick setup from environment variable (OPENROUTER_API_KEY) let client = OpenRouterClient::from_env()?; // Or directly from API key // let client = OpenRouterClient::from_api_key("sk-or-v1-...")?; let request = ChatCompletionRequest { model: "openai/gpt-4o".to_string(), messages: vec![Message::text(ChatRole::User, "Hello, world!")], ..Default::default() }; let response = client.chat()?.chat_completion(request).await?; if let Some(choice) = response.choices.first() { println!("Response: {:?}", choice.message.content); } Ok(()) } ``` #### Multimodal Chat (Audio & PDF) [Permalink: Multimodal Chat (Audio & PDF)](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#multimodal-chat-audio--pdf) ``` use openrouter_api::{OpenRouterClient, Result}; use openrouter_api::types::chat::{ ChatCompletionRequest, ChatRole, Message, MessageContent, ContentPart, TextContent, AudioContent, AudioUrl, FileContent, FileUrl, ContentType, }; #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<()> { let client = OpenRouterClient::from_env()?; let request = ChatCompletionRequest { model: "openai/gpt-4o".to_string(), messages: vec![Message {\ role: ChatRole::User,\ content: MessageContent::Parts(vec![\ ContentPart::Text(TextContent {\ text: "Analyze this audio and document".to_string(),\ }),\ ContentPart::Audio(AudioContent {\ content_type: ContentType::AudioUrl,\ audio_url: AudioUrl {\ url: "https://example.com/audio.mp3".to_string(),\ },\ }),\ ContentPart::File(FileContent {\ content_type: ContentType::FileUrl,\ file_url: FileUrl {\ url: "https://example.com/document.pdf".to_string(),\ },\ }),\ ]),\ ..Default::default()\ }], ..Default::default() }; let response = client.chat()?.chat_completion(request).await?; Ok(()) } ``` #### Routing Shortcuts & Web Search [Permalink: Routing Shortcuts & Web // Add provider preferences and build the payload let payload = request_builder .with_provider_preferences(preferences)? .build(); // The payload now includes provider preferences! println!("Request payload: {}", serde_json::to_string_pretty(&payload)?); use openrouter_api::types::completion::CompletionRequest; use serde_json::json; #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<()> { // Load API key from environment let api_key = utils::load_api_key_from_env()?; use openrouter_api::types::chat::{ChatCompletionRequest, Message}; use futures::StreamExt; use std::io::Write; #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<()> { // Load API key from environment let api_key = utils::load_api_key_from_env()?; // Build the client let client = OpenRouterClient::new() .with_base_url("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/")? .with_api_key(api_key)?; // Create a chat completion request with streaming enabled let request = ChatCompletionRequest { model: "openai/gpt-4o".to_string(), messages: vec![Message {\ role: "user".to_string(),\ content: "Tell me a story.".to_string(),\ name: None,\ tool_calls: None,\ }], stream: Some(true), response_format: None, tools: None, provider: None, models: None, transforms: None, }; // Invoke the streaming chat completion endpoint let chat_api = client.chat()?; let mut stream = chat_api.chat_completion_stream(request); // Process the stream - accumulating content and tracking usage let mut total_content = String::new(); while let Some(chunk) = stream.next().await { match chunk { Ok(c) => { if let Some(choice) = c.choices.first() { if let Some(content) = &choice.delta.content { print!("{}", content); total_content.push_str(content); std::io::stdout().flush().unwrap(); } } // Check for usage information in final chunk if let Some(usage) = c.usage { println!("\nUsage: {} prompt + {} completion = {} total tokens", usage.prompt_tokens, usage.completion_tokens, usage.total_tokens); } }, Err(e) => eprintln!("Error during streaming: {}", e), } } println!(); Ok(()) } ``` #### Analytics API Example [Permalink: Analytics API Example](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#analytics-api-example) ``` use openrouter_api::{OpenRouterClient, utils, Result}; use openrouter_api::types::analytics::{AnalyticsQuery, ActivityType, DateRange}; #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<()> { // Load API key from environment let api_key = utils::load_api_key_from_env()?; // Build the client let client = OpenRouterClient::new() .with_base_url("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/")? .with_api_key(api_key)?; // Get the analytics API let analytics_api = client.analytics()?; // Example 1: Get all activity data with pagination let mut all_activities = Vec::new(); let mut page = 1; loop { let query = AnalyticsQuery::new() .with_page(page) .with_per_page(100); let response = analytics_api.query(query).await?; all_activities.extend(response.data); if response.data.len() < 100 { break; // Last page } page += 1; } println!("Retrieved {} total activities", all_activities.len()); // Example 2: Filter by specific activity types let chat_query = AnalyticsQuery::new() .with_activity_type(vec![ActivityType::ChatCompletion]) .with_per_page(50); let chat_response = analytics_api.query(chat_query).await?; println!("Found {} chat completion activities", chat_response.data.len()); // Example 3: Get activity within a date range let date_range_query = AnalyticsQuery::new() .with_date_range(DateRange::Custom { start: "2024-01-01".to_string(), end: "2024-01-31".to_string(), }); let january_response = analytics_api.query(date_range_query).await?; println!("January activities: {}", january_response.data.len()); // Example 4: Get usage statistics let usage_stats = analytics_api.usage().await?; println!("Total requests: {}", usage_stats.total_requests); println!("Total tokens: {}", usage_stats.total_tokens); // Example 5: Get daily activity for the last 7 days let daily_activity = analytics_api.daily_activity().await?; for day in daily_activity { println!("{}: {} requests, {} tokens", day.date, day.request_count, day.token_count); } Ok(()) } ``` #### Providers API Example [Permalink: Providers API Example](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#providers-api-example) ``` use openrouter_api::{OpenRouterClient, utils, Result}; use openrouter_api::types::providers::{ProvidersQuery, ProviderSort}; #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<()> { // Load API key from environment let api_key = utils::load_api_key_from_env()?; // Build the client let client = OpenRouterClient::new() .with_base_url("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/")? .with_api_key(api_key)?; // Get the providers API let providers_api = client.providers()?; // Example 1: List all available providers let all_providers = providers_api.list().await?; println!("Found {} providers", all_providers.len()); for provider in &all_providers { println!("{}: {} models", provider.name, provider.model_count); } // Example 2: Search for specific providers let search_query = ProvidersQuery::new() .with_search("openai") .with_sort(ProviderSort::Name); let search_results = providers_api.search(search_query).await?; println!("Found {} providers matching 'openai'", search_results.len()); // Example 3: Get provider by name if let Some(openai) = providers_api.get_by_name("OpenAI").await? { println!("OpenAI provider details:"); println!(" Models: {}", openai.model_count); println!(" Status: {:?}", openai.status); // Extract domain from provider's first model URL if let Some(first_model) = openai.models.first() { if let Some(domain) = first_model.extract_domain() { println!(" Domain: {}", domain); } } } // Example 4: Get providers with specific capabilities let capability_query = ProvidersQuery::new() .with_capability("chat"); let chat_providers = providers_api.query(capability_query).await?; println!("{} providers support chat", chat_providers.len()); Ok(()) } ``` #### Enhanced Models API Example [Permalink: Enhanced Models API Example](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#enhanced-models-api-example) ``` use openrouter_api::{OpenRouterClient, utils, Result}; println!(" Pricing: ${}/1M tokens", gpt4.pricing.prompt); if let Some(description) = gpt4.description { println!(" Description: {}", description); } } Ok(()) } ``` ## Model Context Protocol (MCP) Client [Permalink: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Client](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#model-context-protocol-mcp-client) The library includes a client implementation for the [Model Context Protocol](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/), which is an open protocol that standardizes how applications provide context to LLMs. Key features of the MCP client include: - **JSON-RPC Communication:** Implements the JSON-RPC 2.0 protocol for MCP - **Resource Access:** Retrieve resources from MCP servers - **Tool Invocation:** Call tools provided by MCP servers - **Prompt Execution:** Execute prompts on MCP servers - **Server Capabilities:** Discover and leverage server capabilities - **Proper Authentication:** Handle initialization and authentication flows ``` // Create an MCP client connected to a server let client = MCPClient::new("https://mcp-server.example.com/mcp")?; // Initialize with client capabilities let server_capabilities = client.initialize(ClientCapabilities { protocolVersion: "2025-03-26".to_string(), supportsSampling: Some(true), }).await?; // Access resources from the server let resource = client.get_resource(GetResourceParams { id: "some-resource-id".to_string(), parameters: None, }).await?; ``` See the [Model Context Protocol specification](https://spec.modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/) for more details. ## Plugins [Permalink: Plugins](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#plugins) OpenRouter server-side plugins are enabled via `Plugin` convenience constructors on `ChatCompletionRequest`: ``` use openrouter_api::types::chat::{ChatCompletionRequest, Plugin}; // Response Healing (Dec 2025): auto-fixes malformed JSON before it reaches your code let request = ChatCompletionRequest { model: "openai/gpt-4o".to_string(), messages: vec![/* ... */], plugins: Some(vec![Plugin::response_healing()]), ..Default::default() }; // Combine plugins let request = ChatCompletionRequest { model: "openai/gpt-4o".to_string(), messages: vec![/* ... */], plugins: Some(vec![Plugin::web_search(), Plugin::response_healing()]), ..Default::default() }; ``` Available constructors: `Plugin::response_healing()`, `Plugin::web_search()`, `Plugin::file_parser()`, `Plugin::context_compression()`. You can also control the plugin enable flag and send plugin-specific top-level fields: ``` use openrouter_api::types::chat::Plugin; use serde_json::json; let plugin = Plugin::web_search() .with_enabled(true) .with_config(json!({ "max_results": 5, "scope": "news" })); ``` ## Reasoning Config [Permalink: Reasoning Config](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#reasoning-config) Control extended thinking effort, summaries, and token budgets for reasoning-capable models: ``` use openrouter_api::types::chat::{ ChatCompletionRequest, ReasoningConfig, ReasoningEffort, ReasoningSummary, }; // Set effort level and request a detailed reasoning summary let request = ChatCompletionRequest { model: "openai/o3".to_string(), messages: vec![/* ... */], reasoning: Some( ReasoningConfig::with_effort(ReasoningEffort::High) .with_summary(ReasoningSummary::Detailed), ), ..Default::default() }; // Or cap the token budget directly let request = ChatCompletionRequest { model: "deepseek/deepseek-r1".to_string(), messages: vec![/* ... */], reasoning: Some(ReasoningConfig::with_max_tokens(4096)), ..Default::default() }; ``` ## Guardrails Management [Permalink: Guardrails Management](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#guardrails-management) OpenRouter Guardrails management endpoints are available through `client.guardrails()?` for management API keys: ``` use openrouter_api::OpenRouterClient; use openrouter_api::types::guardrails::{ BulkAssignKeysRequest, GuardrailCreateRequest, GuardrailResetInterval, }; #[tokio::main] async fn main() -> Result<(), Box> { let client = OpenRouterClient::from_env()?; let created = client .guardrails()? .create( &GuardrailCreateRequest::new("Production Guardrail") .with_limit_usd(100.0) .with_reset_interval(GuardrailResetInterval::Monthly) .with_enforce_zdr(true), ) .await?; client .guardrails()? .bulk_assign_keys( &created.data.id, &BulkAssignKeysRequest::new(vec!["key-hash-123".to_string()]), ) .await?; Ok(()) } ``` ## Implementation Status [Permalink: Implementation **Type‑State Client Configuration:** The client is built using a type‑state pattern to ensure that required parameters are set before making any API calls. - **Provider Preferences:** Strongly-typed configuration for model routing, fallbacks, and provider selection. - **Asynchronous Streaming:** Support for streaming responses via asynchronous streams. - **Model Context Protocol:** Client implementation for connecting to MCP servers to access resources, tools, and prompts. - **Error Handling & Validation:** Comprehensive error handling with detailed context and validation utilities. * * * ## Architecture & Module Overview [Permalink: Architecture & Module Overview](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#architecture--module-overview) The crate is organized into several modules: - **`client`:** Type-state client implementation with builder pattern - **`api`:** API endpoint implementations (chat, completions, web search, etc.) - **`models`:** Domain models for structured outputs, provider preferences, tools - **`types`:** Type definitions for requests and responses - **`mcp`:** Model Context Protocol client implementation - **`error`:** Centralized error handling - **`utils`:** Utility functions and helpers * * * ## Client Setup & Type‑State Pattern [Permalink: Client Setup & Type‑State Pattern](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#client-setup--typestate-pattern) ``` // Quick setup (recommended for most use cases) let client = OpenRouterClient::from_env()?; // Production setup with optimized settings let client = OpenRouterClient::production( "sk-or-v1-...", "My App", "https://myapp.com" )?; // Full control with type-state pattern let client = OpenRouterClient::new() .with_base_url("https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/")? .with_timeout(Duration::from_secs(30)) .with_http_referer("https://your-app.com/") .with_api_key(std::env::var("OPENROUTER_API_KEY")?)?; ``` ## API None, tools: None, provider: None, models: None, transforms: None, } ).await?; ``` ### Tool Calling [Permalink: Tool Calling](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#tool-calling) ``` // Define MCPClient::new("https://mcp-server.example.com/mcp")?; // Initialize with client capabilities let server_capabilities = mcp_client.initialize(ClientCapabilities { protocolVersion: MCP_PROTOCOL_VERSION.to_string(), supportsSampling: Some(true), }).await?; // Access a resource from the MCP server let resource = mcp_client.get_resource(GetResourceParams { id: "document-123".to_string(), parameters: None, }).await?; ``` ## Error Handling [Permalink: Error Handling](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#error-handling) The library provides enterprise-grade error handling with automatic retries and consistent behavior across all endpoints. ### Standardized Retry Logic [Permalink: Standardized Retry Logic](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#standardized-retry-logic) All API endpoints automatically retry failed requests with: - **Exponential Backoff:** Starting at 500ms, doubling up to 10 seconds maximum - **Jitter:** ±25% random variation to prevent thundering herd effects - **Smart Status Codes:** Retries on rate limiting (429) and server errors (500, 502, 503, 504) - **Configurable Limits:** Customizable maximum retries and backoff settings ``` use openrouter_api::{OpenRouterClient, Result}; // Custom retry configuration let client = OpenRouterClient::from_env()? .with_retry_config(RetryConfig { max_retries: 5, initial_backoff_ms: 1000, max_backoff_ms: 30000, retry_on_status_codes: vec![429, 500, 502, 503, 504], })?; // Automatic retries happen transparently let response = client.chat()?.chat_completion(request).await?; ``` ### Error Types [Permalink: Error Types](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#error-types) ``` match client.chat()?.chat_completion(request).await { Ok(response) => { println!("Success: {}", response.choices[0].message.content); }, Err(e) => match e { Error::ApiError { code, message, .. } => { eprintln!("API Error ({}): {}", code, message); }, Error::RateLimitExceeded(msg) => { eprintln!("Rate limit exceeded: {}", msg); // Automatic retry with exponential backoff }, Error::HttpError(ref err) if err.is_timeout() => { eprintln!("Request timed out!"); // Automatic retry with exponential backoff }, Error::ConfigError(msg) => { eprintln!("Configuration error: {}", msg); }, Error::ContextLengthExceeded { model, message } => { eprintln!("Context limit exceeded for {}: {}", model, message); }, _ => eprintln!("Other error: {:?}", e), } } ``` ### Retry Behavior [Permalink: Retry Behavior](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#retry-behavior) The library automatically handles: - **Network Timeouts:** Retries with increasing delays - **Rate Limiting:** Respects HTTP 429 with exponential backoff - **Server Errors:** Retries 5xx errors to handle temporary failures - **Connection Issues:** Retries connection failures and DNS errors All retry attempts are logged with operation context for debugging: ``` Retrying chat_completion request (1/3) after 625 ms (base: 500 ms, jitter: 25.00%) due to status code 429 Retrying chat_completion request (2/3) after 1250 ms (base: 1000 ms, jitter: 25.00%) due to status code 429 ``` ## Best Practices [Permalink: Best Practices](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#best-practices) 1. **Use the Type‑State Pattern:** Let the compiler ensure your client is properly configured. 2. **Configure Retry Behavior:** Adjust retry settings based on your application's needs: ``` let client = OpenRouterClient::from_env()? .with_retries(5)? // More retries for resilience .without_retries()?; // Disable for testing ``` 3. **Set Appropriate Timeouts & Headers:** Configure reasonable timeouts and identify your application. 4. **Handle Errors Appropriately:** Implement proper error handling for each error type. The automatic retry logic handles most transient failures. 5. **Use Provider Preferences:** Configure provider routing for optimal model selection. 6. **Monitor Retry Behavior:** Watch retry logs in production to identify patterns and adjust configuration. 7. **Secure Your API Keys:** Store keys in environment variables or secure storage. ## Additional Resources [Permalink: Additional Resources](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#additional-resources) - [OpenRouter API Documentation](https://openrouter.ai/docs) - [Model Context Protocol Specification](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/specification/2025-03-26/) * * * ## About Rust API for openrouter. ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#readme-ov-file) ### License [MIT license](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#MIT-1-ov-file) ### Contributing [Contributing](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api#contributing-ov-file) ### Uh oh! There was an error while loading. [Please reload this page](https://github.com/socrates8300/openrouter_api). Repository files navigation # OpenRouter Function Calling & Structured Output Guide [Permalink: OpenRouter Function Calling & Structured Output Guide](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#openrouter-function-calling--structured-output-guide) A comprehensive guide to implementing function calling and structured output with OpenRouter API using the OpenAI SDK, Instructor, and Pydantic. * * * ## 📋 Table of Contents [Permalink: 📋 Table of Contents](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-table-of-contents) - [Introduction](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-introduction) - [What is Function Calling?](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-what-is-function-calling) - [What is Structured Output?](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-what-is-structured-output) - [Installation](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-installation) - [Quick Start](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-quick-start) - [Architecture Overview](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-architecture-overview) - [Examples](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-examples) - [Example 1: Basic Calculator](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-1-basic-calculator) - [Example 2: Weather API](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-2-weather-api) - [Example 3: Multiple Tools](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-3-multiple-tools) - [Example 4: Structured Output](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-4-structured-output) - [Example 5: Nested Structures](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-5-nested-structures) - [Example 6: SQL Query Generator](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-6-sql-query-generator) - [Example 7: Chain of Thought Math Solver](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-7-chain-of-thought-math-solver) - [Model Selection Guide](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-model-selection-guide) - [Best Practices](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-best-practices) - [Troubleshooting](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-troubleshooting) - [Contributing](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-contributing) - [References](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-references) * * * ## 🚀 Introduction [Permalink: 🚀 Introduction](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-introduction) This project demonstrates how to leverage **OpenRouter's API** to implement powerful AI features: 1. **Function Calling**: Enable AI models to intelligently select and execute functions based on user queries 2. **Structured Output**: Extract and validate data using Pydantic models for type-safe, structured responses OpenRouter provides access to multiple AI models through a unified API compatible with OpenAI's SDK, making it easy to experiment with different models while keeping your code consistent. ### Why This Matters [Permalink: Why This Matters](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#why-this-matters) - **Function Calling** transforms static AI responses into dynamic interactions with external tools and APIs - **Structured Output** ensures reliable data extraction with proper validation and type safety - **OpenRouter** gives you access to various models (including free tiers) without vendor lock-in * * * ## 🤖 What is Function Calling? [Permalink: 🤖 What is Function Calling?](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-what-is-function-calling) Function calling allows AI models to: 1. Understand when a user's request requires external tools or data 2. Select the appropriate function to call 3. Extract parameters from natural language 4. Return structured arguments for function execution ### How It Works [Permalink: How It Works](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#how-it-works) ``` User: "What's 42 multiplied by 17?" ↓ AI determines: Need calculator function ↓ AI extracts: { "a": 42, "b": 17, "operation": "multiply" } ↓ Your code executes: calculate(42, 17, "multiply") ↓ Returns: 714 ↓ AI responds: "42 multiplied by 17 equals 714" ``` ### Real-World Use Cases [Permalink: Real-World Use Cases](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#real-world-use-cases) - 🌤️ **Weather Apps**: "What's the weather in Tokyo?" → Calls weather API - 🗃️ **Database Queries**: "Show me all users" → Executes database search - 🧮 **Calculations**: "Calculate 15% tip on $85" → Performs math operations - 🕐 **Time Zones**: "What time is it in London?" → Fetches time data * * * ## 📊 What is Structured Output? [Permalink: 📊 What is Structured Output?](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-what-is-structured-output) Structured output uses **Pydantic models** to: 1. Define expected data structures with type hints 2. Validate AI-generated data automatically 3. Handle nested objects and complex relationships 4. Provide IDE autocomplete and type checking ### How It Works [Permalink: How It Works](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#how-it-works-1) ``` from pydantic import BaseModel class User(BaseModel): name: str age: int email: str # AI extracts from: "My name is Sarah, I'm 28, email is sarah@example.com" user = instructor_client.chat.completions.create( model="...", response_model=User, messages=[...] ) # Result: User(name="Sarah", age=28, email="sarah@example.com") ``` ### Real-World Use Cases [Permalink: Real-World Use Cases](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#real-world-use-cases-1) - 👤 **User Profile Extraction**: Parse user information from text - 🛒 **E-commerce**: Extract product details, orders, pricing - 📝 **SQL Generation**: Convert natural language to validated SQL queries - 🧮 **Problem Solving**: Structure step-by-step reasoning * * * ## 🛠 Installation [Permalink: 🛠 Installation](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-installation) ### Prerequisites [Permalink: Prerequisites](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#prerequisites) - Python 3.9 or higher - OpenRouter API key ( [Get one free](https://openrouter.ai/)) ### Setup Steps [Permalink: Setup Steps](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#setup-steps) 1. **Clone or download this project** ``` cd open_router_function_calling_structured_output ``` 2. **Create a virtual environment** (recommended) ``` python -m venv venv source venv/bin/activate # On Windows: venv\Scripts\activate ``` 3. **Install dependencies** ``` pip install -r requirements.txt ``` 4. **Configure your API key** ``` cp .env.example .env ``` Edit `.env` and add your OpenRouter API key: ``` OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your_api_key_here ``` 5. **Verify installation** ``` python examples/01_basic_calculator.py ``` * * * ## ⚡ Quick Start [Permalink: ⚡ Quick Start](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-quick-start) ### Minimal Function Calling Example [Permalink: Minimal Function Calling Example](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#minimal-function-calling-example) ``` from config import client, DEFAULT_MODEL from tools.functions import CALCULATOR_TOOLS from utils import run_function_calling_conversation # Ask a question that requires calculation response = run_function_calling_conversation( user_message="What is 15 multiplied by 8?", tools=CALCULATOR_TOOLS ) ``` ### Minimal Structured Output Example [Permalink: Minimal Structured Output Example](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#minimal-structured-output-example) ``` import instructor from config import client, DEFAULT_MODEL from models.schemas import User instructor_client = instructor.from_openai(client, mode=instructor.Mode.TOOLS) user = instructor_client.chat.completions.create( model=DEFAULT_MODEL, response_model=User, messages=[{\ "role": "user",\ "content": "My name is John, I'm 30, email is john@example.com"\ }] ) print(f"Name: {user.name}, Age: {user.age}") ``` * * * ## 🏗 Architecture Overview [Permalink: 🏗 Architecture Overview](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-architecture-overview) ### Project Structure [Permalink: Project Structure](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#project-structure) ``` open_router_function_calling_structured_output/ ├── config.py # OpenRouter client configuration ├── utils.py # Conversation orchestration ├── requirements.txt # Python dependencies ├── .env.example # Environment template ├── .gitignore # Git ignore rules ├── models/ │ └── schemas.py # Pydantic models for structured output ├── tools/ │ └── functions.py # Tool definitions and implementations └── examples/ ├── 01_basic_calculator.py ├── 02_weather_api.py ├── 03_multiple_tools.py ├── 04_structured_output.py ├── 05_nested_structures.py ├── 06_sql_generator.py └── 07_math_solver.py ``` ### Key Components [Permalink: Key Components](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#key-components) #### 1\. Configuration (`config.py`) [Permalink: 1. Configuration (config.py)](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#1-configuration-configpy) - Initializes OpenRouter client with OpenAI SDK - Loads API key from environment - Sets default model #### 2\. Tools (`tools/functions.py`) [Permalink: 2. Tools (tools/functions.py)](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#2-tools-toolsfunctionspy) - **Function Implementations**: Actual Python functions (calculate, get\_weather, etc.) - **Tool Schemas**: OpenAI-compatible function definitions - **Function Registry**: Maps function names to implementations #### 3\. Models (`models/schemas.py`) [Permalink: 3. Models (models/schemas.py)](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#3-models-modelsschemaspy) - **Pydantic Models**: Type-safe data structures - **Field Validators**: Handle edge cases like JSON string parsing - **Nested Structures**: Complex relationships (e.g., Order with Address and Items) #### 4\. Utils (`utils.py`) [Permalink: 4. Utils (utils.py)](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#4-utils-utilspy) - **Conversation Flow**: Orchestrates message exchange - **Tool Execution**: Dynamically calls functions based on AI decisions - **Response Synthesis**: Combines tool results into final answer * * * ## 📚 Examples [Permalink: 📚 Examples](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-examples) ### Example 1: Basic Calculator [Permalink: Example 1: Basic Calculator](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-1-basic-calculator) **File**: `examples/01_basic_calculator.py` **What it demonstrates**: - Simple function calling - Single tool usage - Parameter extraction from natural language **Run it**: ``` python examples/01_basic_calculator.py ``` **Expected Output**: ``` Question: What is 42 multiplied by 17? Function called: calculate(a=42, b=17, operation='multiply') Result: 714 AI: 42 multiplied by 17 equals 714 ``` **Key Concepts**: - AI identifies need for calculation - Extracts numbers and operation from text - Returns structured arguments: `{"a": 42, "b": 17, "operation": "multiply"}` * * * ### Example 2: Weather API [Permalink: Example 2: Weather API](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-2-weather-api) **File**: `examples/02_weather_api.py` **What it demonstrates**: - Calling external APIs (mocked) - Handling location parameters - Natural language to function arguments **Run it**: ``` python examples/02_weather_api.py ``` **Sample Interaction**: ``` Q: What's the weather like in Tokyo? → Calls: get_weather(location="Tokyo") → AI Response: "It's sunny in Tokyo with a temperature of 22°C" ``` * * * ### Example 3: Multiple Tools [Permalink: Example 3: Multiple Tools](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-3-multiple-tools) **File**: `examples/03_multiple_tools.py` **What it demonstrates**: - AI choosing from multiple available tools - Sequential function calls - Combining results from different functions **Run it**: ``` python examples/03_multiple_tools.py ``` **Sample Interaction**: ``` Q: What time is it in Tokyo and what's the weather there? → Calls: get_current_time(timezone="Asia/Tokyo") → Calls: get_weather(location="Tokyo") → AI Response: "It's 3:45 PM in Tokyo and the weather is sunny at 22°C" ``` **Key Concepts**: - AI determines multiple functions needed - Executes them in logical order - Synthesizes results into coherent response * * * ### Example 4: Structured Output [Permalink: Example 4: Structured Output](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-4-structured-output) **File**: `examples/04_structured_output.py` **What it demonstrates**: - Extracting data with Pydantic models - Type validation - Handling lists and optional fields **Run it**: ``` python examples/04_structured_output.py ``` **User Model Example**: ``` Input: "My name is Sarah, I'm 28, email is sarah@example.com. I love hiking and photography." Output: User( name="Sarah Johnson", age=28, email="sarah@example.com", interests=["hiking", "photography"] ) ``` **Product Model Example**: ``` Input: "The UltraBook Pro 15 is priced at $1,899. Features: 4K display, 32GB RAM, 1TB SSD. Rated 4.7/5." Output: Product( name="UltraBook Pro 15", category="Electronics", price=1899.0, currency="USD", rating=4.7, features=["4K display", "32GB RAM", "1TB SSD"] ) ``` * * * ### Example 5: Nested Structures [Permalink: Example 5: Nested Structures](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-5-nested-structures) **File**: `examples/05_nested_structures.py` **What it demonstrates**: - Complex nested Pydantic models - Relationships between objects - Calculated properties **Run it**: ``` python examples/05_nested_structures.py ``` **Order Structure**: ``` Order ├── order_id: str ├── customer_name: str ├── customer_email: str ├── shipping_address: Address │ ├── street: str │ ├── city: str │ ├── state: str │ ├── zip_code: str │ └── country: str ├── items: List[OrderItem] │ └── [product_name, quantity, price_per_unit, total_price] ├── total_amount: float (computed) └── status: str ``` **Why This Matters**: - Handles real-world e-commerce data - Validates nested relationships - Automatically calculates totals * * * ### Example 6: SQL Query Generator [Permalink: Example 6: SQL Query Generator](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-6-sql-query-generator) **File**: `examples/06_sql_generator.py` **What it demonstrates**: - Natural language to SQL conversion - Schema-aware query generation - Explanation and complexity estimation **Run it**: ``` python examples/06_sql_generator.py ``` **Sample Queries**: **Input**: "Show me all active users who signed up in the last 30 days" **Output**: ``` SELECT * FROM users WHERE active = TRUE AND created_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '30 days' ORDER BY created_at DESC; ``` **Structured Response Includes**: - `query_type`: "SELECT" - `tables_used`: \["users"\] - `estimated_complexity`: "simple" - `explanation`: Detailed breakdown of what the query does * * * ### Example 7: Chain of Thought Math Solver [Permalink: Example 7: Chain of Thought Math Solver](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#example-7-chain-of-thought-math-solver) **File**: `examples/07_math_solver.py` **What it demonstrates**: - Step-by-step reasoning - Structured thought process - Verification and validation **Run it**: ``` python examples/07_math_solver.py ``` **Problem**: ``` A train travels from City A to City B at 80 km/h. Return journey at 100 km/h. Total time: 4.5 hours. What is the distance between cities? ``` **Structured Solution**: ``` MathProblemSolution( problem="...", thought_process=ThoughtProcess( understanding="Need to find distance using time and speed", approach="Use time = distance/speed formula for both directions", steps=[\ "Let distance = d",\ "Time to B: d/80 hours",\ "Time back: d/100 hours",\ "Total: d/80 + d/100 = 4.5",\ ...\ ], assumptions=["Constant speed", "No stops"] ), solution_steps=["Combine fractions...", "Solve for d..."], final_answer="200 km", verification="Check: 200/80 + 200/100 = 2.5 + 2 = 4.5 ✓" ) ``` **Why This Matters**: - Transparent reasoning process - Educational value (shows working) - Verifiable solutions * * * ## 🎯 Model Selection Guide [Permalink: 🎯 Model Selection Guide](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-model-selection-guide) OpenRouter provides access to many models. Here's guidance on choosing: ### Free Tier Models (Good for Testing) [Permalink: Free Tier Models (Good for Testing)](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#free-tier-models-good-for-testing) ``` # Fast, good for simple tasks "mistralai/mistral-7b-instruct:free" # Alternative free option "meta-llama/llama-3.2-3b-instruct:free" ``` ### Recommended Paid Models [Permalink: Recommended Paid Models](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#recommended-paid-models) ``` # Best overall (function calling + structured output) "anthropic/claude-3.5-sonnet" # Good balance of performance and cost "openai/gpt-4o-mini" # Maximum capability "openai/gpt-4o" ``` ### Model-Specific Considerations [Permalink: Model-Specific Considerations](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#model-specific-considerations) | Model | Function Calling | Structured Output | Notes | | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Claude 3.5 Sonnet | ✅ Excellent | ✅ Excellent | Best for complex reasoning | | GPT-4o | ✅ Excellent | ✅ Excellent | Great all-rounder | | GPT-4o-mini | ✅ Good | ✅ Good | Cost-effective | | Mistral 7B | ⚠️ Basic | ⚠️ Needs validators | Free but may return JSON strings | ### Known Issues [Permalink: Known Issues](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#known-issues) **Problem**: Some free models (like `kwaipilot/kat-coder-pro:free`) return nested objects as JSON strings instead of proper objects. **Solution**: Field validators are included in `models/schemas.py`: ``` @field_validator('shipping_address', mode='before') @classmethod def parse_shipping_address(cls, v): if isinstance(v, str): return json.loads(v) return v ``` * * * ## ✅ Best Practices [Permalink: ✅ Best Practices](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-best-practices) ### Function Calling [Permalink: Function Calling](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#function-calling) 1. **Clear Function Descriptions** ``` { "name": "calculate", "description": "Perform arithmetic operations (add, subtract, multiply, divide)", # ✅ Clear and specific } ``` 2. **Detailed Parameter Descriptions** ``` "parameters": { "type": "object", "properties": { "a": {"type": "number", "description": "First number"}, "operation": { "type": "string", "enum": ["add", "subtract", "multiply", "divide"], "description": "Mathematical operation to perform" } } } ``` 3. **Error Handling** ``` def calculate(a, b, operation): try: if operation == "divide" and b == 0: return "Error: Division by zero" # ... perform calculation except Exception as e: return f"Error: {str(e)}" ``` ### Structured Output [Permalink: Structured Output](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#structured-output) 1. **Use Type Hints** ``` class User(BaseModel): name: str # ✅ Required field age: Optional[int] # ✅ Optional field interests: List[str] = [] # ✅ Default value ``` 2. **Add Validators for Edge Cases** ``` @field_validator('rating', mode='before') @classmethod def parse_rating(cls, v): if isinstance(v, str): return float(v) return v ``` 3. **Document Your Models** ``` class Product(BaseModel): """Product information extracted from text. Attributes: name: Product name price: Price in the specified currency rating: Customer rating (0-5 scale) """ name: str price: float rating: float ``` ### Security [Permalink: Security](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#security) 1. **Never expose API keys** - Use `.env` files (not in git) - Validate on server side - Rotate keys regularly 2. **Validate Function Calls** ``` # Whitelist allowed functions ALLOWED_FUNCTIONS = ["calculate", "get_weather"] if function_name not in ALLOWED_FUNCTIONS: raise ValueError(f"Function {function_name} not allowed") ``` 3. **Sanitize User Input** ``` # Before passing to database query = escape_sql(user_input) ``` * * * ## 🔧 Troubleshooting [Permalink: 🔧 Troubleshooting](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-troubleshooting) ### Issue 1: ValidationError for Nested Objects [Permalink: Issue 1: ValidationError for Nested Objects](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#issue-1-validationerror-for-nested-objects) **Error**: ``` ValidationError: Field 'shipping_address' expected dict but got str ``` **Cause**: Model returning JSON string instead of object **Solution**: Field validator already included in `models/schemas.py`: ``` @field_validator('shipping_address', mode='before') @classmethod def parse_shipping_address(cls, v): if isinstance(v, str): return json.loads(v) return v ``` ### Issue 2: Model Not Found [Permalink: Issue 2: Model Not Found](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#issue-2-model-not-found) **Error**: ``` NotFoundError: model 'xxx' not found ``` **Cause**: Model name incorrect or not available **Solution**: Check [OpenRouter Models](https://openrouter.ai/models) for available models ### Issue 3: Function Not Called [Permalink: Issue 3: Function Not Called](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#issue-3-function-not-called) **Symptom**: AI responds without calling function **Possible Causes**: 1. Function description unclear 2. Model doesn't support function calling well 3. Question doesn't clearly need the function **Solutions**: - Improve function description - Try a better model (Claude, GPT-4) - Make the user query more explicit ### Issue 4: Rate Limiting [Permalink: Issue 4: Rate Limiting](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#issue-4-rate-limiting) **Error**: ``` RateLimitError: Rate limit exceeded ``` **Solutions**: - Add delays between requests: `time.sleep(1)` - Implement exponential backoff - Consider upgrading OpenRouter plan * * * ## 🤝 Contributing [Permalink: 🤝 Contributing](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-contributing) Contributions are welcome! Areas for improvement: 1. **More Examples**: Add real-world use cases 2. **New Tools**: Implement additional function tools 3. **Model Comparisons**: Test and document different models 4. **Error Handling**: Improve robustness 5. **Documentation**: Expand guides and tutorials * * * ## 📖 References [Permalink: 📖 References](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-references) ### Official Documentation [Permalink: Official Documentation](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#official-documentation) - [OpenRouter Documentation](https://openrouter.ai/docs) - [OpenAI Function Calling Guide](https://platform.openai.com/docs/guides/function-calling) - [Pydantic V2 Documentation](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/) - [Instructor Library](https://python.useinstructor.com/) ### Related Resources [Permalink: Related Resources](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#related-resources) - [OpenRouter Model Comparison](https://openrouter.ai/models) - [Pydantic Best Practices](https://docs.pydantic.dev/latest/concepts/models/) - [Python dotenv](https://pypi.org/project/python-dotenv/) ### Community [Permalink: Community](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#community) - [OpenRouter Discord](https://discord.gg/openrouter) - [Pydantic GitHub](https://github.com/pydantic/pydantic) - [Instructor GitHub](https://github.com/jxnl/instructor) * * * ## 📄 License [Permalink: 📄 License](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-license) This project is provided as-is for educational purposes. Feel free to use, modify, and distribute. * * * ## 🙏 Acknowledgments [Permalink: 🙏 Acknowledgments](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-acknowledgments) - **OpenRouter** for providing unified API access - **Instructor** for simplifying structured output - **Pydantic** for robust data validation - The **OpenAI** team for the excellent SDK and function calling design * * * ## 💡 Next Steps [Permalink: 💡 Next Steps](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#-next-steps) 1. ✅ Complete the installation 2. ✅ Run all 7 examples 3. ✅ Experiment with different models 4. ✅ Build your own tools and models 5. ✅ Deploy in production applications **Happy coding! 🚀** * * * _Last Updated: 2024_ ## About Guide to implementing function calling and structured output with OpenRouter API ### Topics [python](https://github.com/topics/python "Topic: python") [structured-output](https://github.com/topics/structured-output "Topic: structured-output") [pydantic](https://github.com/topics/pydantic "Topic: pydantic") [llm](https://github.com/topics/llm "Topic: llm") [function-calling](https://github.com/topics/function-calling "Topic: function-calling") [openrouter](https://github.com/topics/openrouter "Topic: openrouter") [openai-sdk](https://github.com/topics/openai-sdk "Topic: openai-sdk") ### Resources [Readme](https://github.com/anurag629/openrouter_function_calling_structured_output#readme-ov-file) ### Uh oh! This Laravel package provides an easy-to-use interface for integrating **[OpenRouter](https://openrouter.ai/)** into your Laravel applications. **OpenRouter** is a unified interface for Large Language Models (LLMs) that allows you to interact with various **[AI models](https://openrouter.ai/models)** through a single API. ## Table of Contents [Permalink: Table of Contributing](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#-contributing) - [📜 License](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#-license) ## 🤖 Requirements [Permalink: 🤖 Requirements](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#-requirements) - **PHP 8.2** or **higher**: Use version `v2.x.x` (latest compatible version) - Uses [spatie laravel-data](https://github.com/spatie/laravel-data) - **PHP 8.1**: Use version `v1.0.9`. - Uses [spatie data-transfer-object](https://github.com/spatie/data-transfer-object) - For correct way to use DTOs, refer to [v1.0.9 README](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/tree/v1.0.9). ## 🏁 Get Started [Permalink: 🏁 Get Started](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#-get-started) You can **install** the package via composer: ``` composer require moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter ``` You can **publish** the **config file** with: ``` php artisan vendor:publish --tag=laravel-openrouter ``` This is the contents of the **published config file**: ``` return [\ 'api_endpoint' => env('OPENROUTER_API_ENDPOINT', 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/'),\ 'api_key' => env('OPENROUTER_API_KEY'),\ 'api_timeout' => env('OPENROUTER_API_TIMEOUT', 20),\ 'title' => env('OPENROUTER_API_TITLE', 'laravel-openrouter'),\ 'referer' => env('OPENROUTER_API_REFERER', 'https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter'),\ ]; ``` ## 🧩 Configuration [Permalink: 🧩 Configuration](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#-configuration) After publishing the package configuration file, you'll need to add the following environment variables to your **.env** file: ``` OPENROUTER_API_ENDPOINT=https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/ OPENROUTER_API_KEY=your_api_key OPENROUTER_API_TIMEOUT=request_timeout OPENROUTER_API_TITLE= OPENROUTER_API_REFERER= ``` Note - `OPENROUTER_API_ENDPOINT`: The endpoint URL for the **OpenRouter API** (default: [https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/](https://openrouter.ai/api/v1/)). - `OPENROUTER_API_KEY`: Your **API key** for accessing the OpenRouter API. You can obtain this key from the [OpenRouter dashboard](https://openrouter.ai/settings/keys). - `OPENROUTER_API_TIMEOUT`: Request timeout in seconds. Increase value to 120 - 180 if you use long-thinking models like openai/o1 (default: 20) - `OPENROUTER_API_TITLE`: Optional - Site URL for rankings on openrouter.ai - `OPENROUTER_API_REFERER`: Optional - Site referer for rankings on openrouter.ai ## 🎨 Usage [Permalink: 🎨 Usage](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#-usage) This package provides two ways to interact with the OpenRouter API: - Using the [`LaravelOpenRouter`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/Facades/LaravelOpenRouter.php) facade - Instantiating the [`OpenRouterRequest`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/OpenRouterRequest.php) class directly. Both methods utilize the [`ChatData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ChatData.php) DTO class to structure the data sent to the API. ### Understanding ChatData DTO [Permalink: Understanding ChatData DTO](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#understanding-chatdata-dto) The [`ChatData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ChatData.php) class is used to **encapsulate the data** required for making chat requests to the OpenRouter API. Here's a breakdown of the key properties: - **messages** (array\|null): An array of [`MessageData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/MessageData.php) objects representing the chat messages. This field is XOR-gated with the `prompt` field. - **prompt** (string\|null): A string representing the prompt for the chat request. This field is XOR-gated with the `messages` field. - **model** (string\|null): The name of the model to be used for the chat request. If not specified, the user's default model will be used. This field is XOR-gated with the `models` field. - **response\_format** (ResponseFormatData\|null): An instance of the [`ResponseFormatData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ResponseFormatData.php) class representing the desired format for the response. - **usage** (bool\|null): A boolean indicating whether to include usage information in the response. Default is `false` because enabling usage accounting will add a few hundred milliseconds to the response as the API calculates token counts and costs. - **stop** (array\|string\|null): A value specifying the stop sequence for the chat generation. - **stream** (bool\|null): A boolean indicating whether streaming should be enabled or not. - **include\_reasoning** (bool\|null): Whether to return the model's reasoning (Note: this parameter is **deprecated**, use `reasoning` parameter instead. For backward compatibility, package still supports the `include_reasoning` parameter) - **reasoning** (ReasoningData\|null): An instance of the [`ReasoningData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ReasoningData.php) class for reasoning configuration. It provides a transparent look into the reasoning steps taken by a model. - **cache\_control** ( [`CacheControlData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/CacheControlData.php) \|null): Controls **prompt caching** on supported providers/models. You can set it at the **top-level** of the request (recommended for multi-turn conversations) or as an explicit **breakpoint** on large text blocks via `TextContentData::$cache_control`. For details and provider-specific behavior, see [OpenRouter Prompt Caching](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/best-practices/prompt-caching). #### LLM Parameters [Permalink: LLM Parameters](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#llm-parameters) These properties control various aspects of the generated response (more [info](https://openrouter.ai/docs/api-reference/parameters)): - **max\_tokens** (int\|null): The maximum number of tokens that can be generated in the completion. Default is 1024. - **temperature** (float\|null): A value between 0 and 2 controlling the randomness of the output. - **top\_p** (float\|null): A value between 0 and 1 for nucleus sampling, an alternative to temperature sampling. - **top\_k** (float\|null): A value between 1 and infinity for top-k sampling (not available for OpenAI models). - **frequency\_penalty** (float\|null): A value between -2 and 2 for penalizing new tokens based on their existing frequency. - **presence\_penalty** (float\|null): A value between -2 and 2 for penalizing new tokens based on whether they appear in the text so far. - **repetition\_penalty** (float\|null): A value between 0 and 2 for penalizing repetitive tokens. - **seed** (int\|null): A value for deterministic sampling (OpenAI models only, in beta). #### Tool-calling Parameters [Permalink: Tool-calling Parameters](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#tool-calling-parameters) Only natively suported by OpenAI models. For others, we submit a YAML-formatted string with these tools at the end of the prompt. - **tool\_choice** (string\|array\|null): A value specifying the tool choice for function calling (OpenAI models only). - **tools** (array\|null): An array of [`ToolCallData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ToolCallData.php) objects for function calling. #### Additional optional parameters [Permalink: Additional optional parameters](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#additional-optional-parameters) - **logit\_bias** (array\|null): An array for modifying the likelihood of specified tokens appearing in the completion. #### OpenRouter-only parameters [Permalink: OpenRouter-only parameters](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#openrouter-only-parameters) - **transforms** (array\|null): An array for configuring prompt transforms. - **plugins** (array\|null): An array of [`PluginData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/PluginData.php) objects for configuring plugins such as `web search` or `file parsing`. - **web\_search\_options** (WebSearchOptionsData\|null): An instance of the [`WebSearchOptionsData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/WebSearchOptionsData.php) DTO object for configuring web search (e.g. `search_context_size: SearchContextSizeType::LOW`). - **models** (array\|null): An array of models to automatically try if the primary model is unavailable. This field is XOR-gated with the `model` field. - **route** (string\|null): A value specifying the route type (e.g., `RouteType::FALLBACK`). - **provider** (ProviderPreferencesData\|null): An instance of the [`ProviderPreferencesData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ProviderPreferencesData.php) DTO object for configuring provider preferences (see [Provider Preferences](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#provider-preferences)). #### Provider Preferences [Permalink: Provider Preferences](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#provider-preferences) The [`ProviderPreferencesData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ProviderPreferencesData.php) DTO allows fine-grained control over provider routing. For more details, see [OpenRouter Provider Selection](https://openrouter.ai/docs/guides/routing/provider-selection). | Property | Type | Description | | --- | --- | --- | | `allow_fallbacks` | `bool|null` | Whether to allow backup providers when the primary is unavailable (default: `true`). | | `require_parameters` | `bool|null` | Only use providers that support all parameters in your request. | | `data_collection` | `string|null` | `DataCollectionType::ALLOW` (default) or `DataCollectionType::DENY`. | | `order` | `array|null` | Ordered list of provider slugs to prefer. | | `zdr` | `bool|null` | Restrict routing to only Zero Data Retention endpoints. | | `enforce_distillable_text` | `bool|null` | Restrict routing to only models that allow text distillation. | | `only` | `array|null` | List of provider slugs to allow for this request. | | `ignore` | `array|null` | List of provider slugs to skip for this request. | | `quantizations` | `array|null` | Filter by quantization levels (see `QuantizationType`). | | `sort` | `string|ProviderSortData|null` | Sort providers by `"price"`, `"throughput"`, or `"latency"`. Can also be a `ProviderSortData` object with `by` and `partition` fields. | | `preferred_min_throughput` | `float|PercentileData|null` | Preferred minimum throughput (tokens/sec). Can be a number or `PercentileData` with percentile cutoffs (`p50`, `p75`, `p90`, `p99`). | | `preferred_max_latency` | `float|PercentileData|null` | Preferred maximum latency (seconds). Can be a number or `PercentileData` with percentile cutoffs. | | `max_price` | `MaxPriceData|null` | Maximum acceptable pricing with `prompt`, `completion`, `request`, and `image` fields (in USD). | **Example with advanced provider preferences:** ``` $provider = new ProviderPreferencesData( allow_fallbacks: true, require_parameters: true, data_collection: DataCollectionType::DENY, only: ['openai', 'anthropic'], quantizations: [QuantizationType::FP16, QuantizationType::BF16], sort: new ProviderSortData( by: ProviderSortType::PRICE, partition: true, ), preferred_min_throughput: new PercentileData( p50: 100.0, p90: 50.0, ), preferred_max_latency: 2.5, max_price: new MaxPriceData( prompt: 0.001, completion: 0.002, ), ); $chatData = new ChatData( messages: [\ new MessageData(\ role: RoleType::USER,\ content: 'Hello!',\ ),\ ], model: 'openai/gpt-4o', provider: $provider, ); ``` ### Creating a ChatData Instance [Permalink: Creating a ChatData Instance](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#creating-a-chatdata-instance) This is a sample chat data instance (Refer to [spatie laravel-data](https://spatie.be/docs/laravel-data/v4/introduction) how to create, use DTOs): ``` $chatData = new ChatData( messages: [\ new MessageData(\ role: RoleType::USER,\ content: [\ new TextContentData(\ type: TextContentData::ALLOWED_TYPE,\ text: 'This is a sample text content.',\ ),\ new ImageContentPartData(\ type: ImageContentPartData::ALLOWED_TYPE,\ image_url: new ImageUrlData(\ url: 'https://example.com/image.jpg',\ detail: 'Sample image',\ ),\ ),\ ],\ ),\ ], response_format: new ResponseFormatData( type: 'json_object', ), usage: true, stop: ['stop_token'], stream: true, reasoning: new ReasoningData( effort: EffortType::HIGH, exclude: false, ), max_tokens: 1024, temperature: 0.7, top_p: 0.9, top_k: 50, frequency_penalty: 0.5, presence_penalty: 0.2, repetition_penalty: 1.2, seed: 42, tool_choice: 'auto', tools: [\ // ToolCallData instances\ ], logit_bias: [\ '50256' => -100,\ ], transforms: ['middle-out'], plugins: [\ new PluginData(\ id: 'web',\ max_results: 3,\ engine: 'native',\ ),\ ], web_search_options: new WebSearchOptionsData( search_context_size: SearchContextSizeType::MEDIUM, ), models: ['model1', 'model2'], route: RouteType::FALLBACK, provider: new ProviderPreferencesData( allow_fallbacks: true, require_parameters: true, data_collection: DataCollectionType::ALLOW, ignore: ['anthropic'], quantizations: [QuantizationType::FP16, QuantizationType::BF16], sort: ProviderSortType::PRICE, max_price: new MaxPriceData( prompt: 0.001, completion: 0.002, ), ), modalities: ['image', 'text'], image_config: new ImageConfigData( aspect_ratio: '16:9', ), ); ``` ### Using Facade [Permalink: Using Facade](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#using-facade) The `LaravelOpenRouter` facade offers a convenient way to make OpenRouter API requests. #### Chat Request [Permalink: Chat Request](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#chat-request) To send a chat request, create an instance of [`ChatData`](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter/blob/master/src/DTO/ChatData.php) and pass it to the `chatRequest` method: ``` $content = 'Tell me a story about a rogue AI that falls in love with its creator.'; // Your desired prompt or content $model = 'mistralai/mistral-7b-instruct:free'; // The OpenRouter model you want to use (https://openrouter.ai/models) $messageData = new MessageData( content: $content, role: RoleType::USER, ); $chatData = new ChatData( messages: [\ $messageData,\ ], model: $model, max_tokens: 100, // Adjust this value as needed ); $chatResponse = LaravelOpenRouter::chatRequest($chatData); // You can convert the response `toArray` if needed (It converts ResponseData DTO to array including the nested DTOs while filtering null values) $responseArray = $chatResponse->toArray(); ``` - #### Stream Chat Request [Permalink: Stream Chat Request](https://github.com/moe-mizrak/laravel-openrouter#stream-chat-request) Streaming chat request is also supported and can be used as following by using **chatStreamRequest** function: ``` $content = 'Tell me a story about a rogue AI that falls in love with its creator.'; // Your desired prompt or content $model = 'mistralai/mistral-7b-instruct:free'; // The OpenRouter model you want to use (https://openrouter.ai/models) $messageData = new MessageData( content: $content, role: RoleType::USER, ); $chatData = new ChatData( messages: [\ $messageData,\ ], model: $model, max_tokens: 100, ); /* * Calls chatStreamRequest ($promise is type of PromiseInterface) */ $promise = LaravelOpenRouter::chatStreamRequest($chatData); // Waits until the promise completes if possible. $stream = $promise->wait(); // $stream is type of GuzzleHttp\Psr7\Stream /* * 1) You can retrieve whole raw response as: - Choose 1) or 2) depending on your case. */ $rawResponseAll = $stream->getContents(); // Instead of chunking streamed response as below - while (! $stream->eof()), it waits and gets raw response all together. $response = LaravelOpenRouter::filterStreamingResponse($rawResponseAll); // Optionally you can use filterStreamingResponse to filter raw streamed response, and map it into array of responseData DTO same as chatRequest response format. // 2) Or Retrieve streamed raw response as it becomes available: while (! $stream->eof()) { $rawResponse = $stream->read(1024); // readByte can be set as desired, for better performance 4096 byte (4kB) can be used. /* * Optionally you can use filterStreamingResponse to filter raw streamed response, and map it into array of responseData DTO same as chatRequest response format. */ $response = LaravelOpenRouter::filterStreamingResponse($rawResponse); } ``` You do **not** need to specify `'stream' = true` in ChatData since `chatStreamRequest` does it for you. Streaming usage example: ``` Route::get('/test/stream', function () { $content = 'Tell me a story about a rogue AI that falls in love with its creator.'; $model = 'deepseek/deepseek-chat-v3.1'; $messageData = new MessageData( content: $content, role: RoleType::USER, ); $chatData = new ChatData( messages: [$messageData], model: $model, max_tokens: 1000, ); return response()->stream(function () use ($chatData) { $promise = LaravelOpenRouter::chatStreamRequest($chatData); $stream = $promise->wait(); while (!$stream->eof()) { $rawResponse = $stream->read(1024); // 1) Print raw streamed response as it becomes available: echo $rawResponse; /* * 2) Optionally you can use filterStreamingResponse to filter raw streamed response, and map it into array of responseData DTO same as chatRequest response format. */ $response = LaravelOpenRouter::filterStreamingResponse($rawResponse); foreach ($response as $responseData) { // Process each responseData as needed echo json_encode($responseData->toArray()) . PHP_EOL; } flush(); } }, 200, [\ 'Content-Type' => 'text/plain; charset=utf-8',\ ]); }); ``` Sample rawResponse: This is the expected sample rawResponse (raw response returned from OpenRouter stream chunk) `$rawResponse`: ``` """ : OPENROUTER PROCESSING\n \n data:
